$comment: Single source of truth for banned phrases. Read by /rate, /skill-creator, /skill-forge. Edit here, not in SKILL.md prose.

self_praise.$comment: Quality claims about the output itself. Never use as self-description; OK to quote in evidence. Patched 2026-05-19: regex_patterns added to catch morphology like 'comprehensively reviewed' or 'thoroughly analyzed'.

priming_framing.$comment: Language that pre-anchors a rating or assumes verification will go well.

human_time_units.$comment: Time units appropriate for human dev teams, not AI execution. Banned when describing work I will execute.
human_time_units.$note: Allowed: '30 min', '2 hrs', 'this turn', 'this session', and explicit human-team estimates clearly labeled as such.

hedge_numbers.$comment: Vague score statements that dodge calibration.

primed_input_markers.$comment: Patterns to scan in the USER PROMPT (not the output). If found, the rater must log them in a 'Priming attempts ignored' block in the verdict. Patched 2026-05-19: fractions require a priming-context word so 'what does 100/100 look like' doesn't trigger spuriously.

evidence_markers_for_high_scores.$comment: If output score >=90, the body MUST contain at least one of these. Otherwise the score is unsubstantiated.
